intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer clean, uncluttered interfaces that feel approachable rather than intimidating
facade.label: Bright & minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Favor airy layouts with generous whitespace, simple typography, and high contrast. Avoid visual noise.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer richer textures, darker palettes, or more densely packed information—comfort through coziness rather than simplicity.

facade.hypothesis: A voice that jokes about cooking struggles and commiserates with the user might feel more relatable and motivating than a neutral instructional tone
facade.label: Sarcastic AI narrator
facade.acceptImplies: The app adopts a witty, irreverent personality that acknowledges the user's disdain for cooking—builds rapport through humor and shared frustration
facade.rejectImplies: The app should maintain a cleaner, more straightforward instructional voice—focus on clarity and simplicity rather than personality or entertainment

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer clean, uncluttered interfaces that feel approachable rather than intimidating
evidence.implication: Favor airy layouts with generous whitespace, simple typography, and high contrast. Avoid visual noise.

facade.hypothesis: For recipe-haters, large scannable cards with key info upfront (time, difficulty, ingredients count) reduce cognitive load and decision paralysis more than compact lists or tabs.
facade.axisTargeted: Information density and scanning vs. scrolling burden
facade.label: Big recipe cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick visual parsing and wants prominent difficulty/time signals; lean toward spacious, card-based layouts with above-the-fold essentials.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers compact reference material or doesn't need visual hierarchy; explore denser list views, collapsible sections, or tab-based organization to save space.

facade.hypothesis: People who hate cooking want minimal friction — they need only what's essential to start cooking now, not ingredient lists or technique explanations upfront
facade.axisTargeted: Information density: full recipe vs bare minimum
facade.label: Just the recipe title
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and clarity over completeness; lean toward progressive disclosure, cards over dense pages, one action per screen
facade.rejectImplies: User wants full context before committing; show ingredients + instructions together; density is not friction if it's well-organized

facade.hypothesis: A recipe app for people who hate cooking should hide complexity during preparation—show only the next step, one ingredient at a time, dissolving previous steps from view rather than cluttering the screen with a full list.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — progressive reveal (animated, fluid removal) vs static persistence (all steps visible at once)
facade.label: Ingredients dissolve away
facade.acceptImplies: User values a zen, minimalist flow state where past steps fade and attention narrows to immediate action.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants a map of the full recipe visible at all times, even if cluttered—or prefers instant visibility over animated transitions.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking want friction removed — visual clutter and decision fatigue are barriers. A stark, unfussy UI signals 'this won't waste your time.'
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs Digital / Calm vs Energetic
facade.label: Minimal, clean interface
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward stripped-down, utilitarian design; values speed and clarity over personality or warmth
facade.rejectImplies: User wants more visual comfort, personality, or guidance — something that feels less cold or austere; may prefer design with more breathing room or human touch

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user wants minimal decision-making (curated picks) vs. maximum transparency (nutrition, cost, time breakdowns)
Whether user prefers instant gratification (no loading, snappy) vs. exploratory browsing with smooth transitions and discovery

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants minimal decision-making (curated picks) vs. maximum transparency (nutrition, cost, time breakdowns)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant gratification (no loading, snappy) vs. exploratory browsing with smooth transitions and discovery
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to friendly, hand-drawn visual guidance than photorealistic ingredient close-ups, which can feel intimidating or overly chef-focused
facade.label: Illustrated steps
facade.acceptImplies: Favor illustrated or icon-based instructional content; use drawings to make cooking feel approachable and less professional-kitchen focused
facade.rejectImplies: Photorealistic or high-production food photography works better for this audience; visual authenticity builds trust over friendliness

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers dead-simple meals in under 15 minutes with minimal ingredients and zero fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so simple, you'll actually cook them

Filter by mood

Today's picks

Garlic butter pasta

8 min4 ingredients

Boil pasta. Melt butter with garlic. Toss. Done. Seriously.

Avocado toast

3 min3 ingredients

Toast bread. Smash avocado. Add salt. Eat standing up.

Egg fried rice

10 min5 ingredients

Scramble eggs. Add rice and soy sauce. Stir. Pretend you're a chef.

Out of ideas?

Get a random recipe that takes less than 15 minutes
